description: The quality of contributing to collective wellbeing, harmony, and flourishing. Goodness is not an abstract moral virtue but the practical, tangible quality of actions and things that support the thriving of the whole.
rationale: The word begins with 'we', immediately grounding goodness in collective perspective—what benefits all, not just one. This flows through 'lo', the sound of connection and flow, acknowledging that goodness moves between beings like a river nourishing all it touches. The final 'a' opens this flow outward, embracing ever-wider circles of care. This three-syllable journey—from collective awareness through flowing benefit to expansive embrace—redefines goodness not as abstract virtue but as lived practice. The liquid sounds and open vowels make the word itself feel generous and welcoming, impossible to speak with closed heart or clenched jaw.
